steps.title: Spot the modifier position
steps.content: After the subject noun “Pascal” comes the blank, which must combine with the prepositional phrase “in software development and educational instruction” to form a modifying phrase describing the noun.

steps.title: Apply the reduced relative clause rule
steps.content: A reduced relative clause uses a past participle to replace “which is [verb].” “Used” is that past participle, standing in for “which is used,” so “Pascal, used in software development and educational instruction” works as the reduced form. The other options use wrong verb forms—an infinitive, a noun, and a present participle—that cannot function in a reduced relative clause. That matches choice B — B is correct.
